09 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Coast Guard seizes 150,000 pounds of cocaine through Operation Pacific Viper, interdicts drug smuggling vessel loaded with over 20,000 pounds of cocaine US Coast Guard 09 Dec 2025 -- The U.S. Coast Guard announced Tuesday it has seized more than 150,000 pounds of cocaine in the Eastern Pacific Ocean since launching Operation Pacific Viper in early August.

  • Indiana National Guard Soldiers arrive in DC Army News 09 Dec 2025 -- Approximately 300 Indiana National Guard Soldiers arrived in Washington Dec. 6 to support the D.C. Safe and Beautiful mission.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • The War Department Unleashes AI on New GenAI.mil Platform DoD 09 Dec 2025 -- The War Department today announced the launch of Google Cloud's Gemini for Government as the first of several frontier AI capabilities to be housed on GenAI.mil, the Department's new bespoke AI platform.
  • Hegseth Introduces Department to New AI Tool DoD News 09 Dec 2025 -- Yesterday, several employees at the Pentagon got a pop-up on their computers inviting them to use a new artificial intelligence tool developed for the War Department. Some were skeptical, wondering if the invitation was part of a cybersecurity test.
  • USS Tripoli, USS Robert Smalls arrive in Da Nang, Vietnam U.S. 7th Fleet 09 Dec 2025 -- Ticonderoga-class guided-missile cruiser USS Robert Smalls (CG 62) and America-class amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli (LHA 7) with embarked elements of the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU), Amphibious Squadron (PHIBRON) 11 and Commander, Task Force (CTF) 76 arrived in Da Nang, Dec. 8, while conducting routine operations in the U.S. 7th Fleet area of operations.
  • U.S. Coast Guard authenticates keel for offshore patrol cutter Pickering US Coast Guard 09 Dec 2025 -- The Coast Guard authenticated the keel for the future Coast Guard Cutter Pickering during a ceremony Monday in Mobile, Alabama.
  • Navy Lays Keel for Future USS Barb US Navy 09 Dec 2025 -- Senior Navy leaders and shipbuilders gathered at HII-Newport News Shipbuilding (NNS) for the keel authentication of the future Virginia-class attack submarine USS Barb (SSN 804), Dec. 9.
  • U.S., Japan Naval Forces Complete Mine Warfare Exercise 3JA 2025 US Navy 09 Dec 2025 -- The U.S. Navy and Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) completed Mine Warfare Exercise (MINEX) 3JA 2025 off the coast of Southern Japan near Aburatsu, Nov. 12-26.
  • Navy Invests $448 Million in AI and Autonomy to Accelerate Shipbuilding US Navy Press Release 09 Dec 2025 -- Secretary of the Navy John Phelan today announced a $448 million strategic investment in the Shipbuilding Operating System (Ship OS) to accelerate the adoption of artificial intelligence and autonomy technologies across the industrial base.
  • California National Guard enters State Partnership Program with Mexico's Secretariat of National Defense Army News 09 Dec 2025 -- The California National Guard and Mexico's Secretariat of National Defense (Secretaría de la Defensa Nacional) held a signing ceremony Oct. 28 to formalize their partnership in the Department of War National Guard Bureau's State Partnership Program, or SPP.

  • Presenting Gold Star medals to Heroes of Russia President of Russia 09 Dec 2025 -- On Heroes of the Fatherland Day, the President presented Gold Star medals to Heroes of Russia. The ceremony took place in the Grand Kremlin Palace's St George Hall.
  • Situation in Arctic Challenging, Deteriorating - Russian Navy Commander-in-Chief Sputnik 09 Dec 2025 -- The situation in the Arctic remains challenging with a trend to deterioration, Russian Navy Commander-in-Chief Aleksandr Moiseyev said on Tuesday.

  • MoD allocates over UAH 1.5 billion to restore the operations of defense industry enterprises Ukraine MOD 09 Dec 2025
  • State Operator for Non-Lethal Acquisition (DOT) expects to sign over 80% of 2026 clothing and gear contracts for the Armed Forces of Ukraine by the end of the year Ukraine MOD 09 Dec 2025
  • Japan's Ministry of Defense shares cybersecurity expertise with Ukraine under the IT Coalition Ukraine MOD 09 Dec 2025

  • Chinese FM responds to Japan's claim that 'China didn't answer hotline' Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- The spokesperson for the Chinese PLA Navy has already made it clear on December 7 that the training airspace and waters were announced in advance. One may refer to the relevant Chinese authorities for further details, said Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun on Tuesday. The remarks were made in response to a question about Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi's claim that Japan does not believe that it has been given flight and navigation information over sea and airspace related to China's navy training in advance, The Paper reported.
  • China, Russia hold 10th joint strategic air patrol over E.China Sea, western Pacific Ocean; simultaneous operation with carrier Liaoning possible: expert Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese and Russian militaries conducted their 10th joint strategic air patrol on Tuesday which involved bombers and fighter jets. A Chinese expert highlighted the enhanced pragmatic defense cooperation between the two countries, noting that the strategic patrol served to deter factors that undermines peace and stability amid 80th anniversary of victory in the Chinese People's War of Resistance against Japanese Aggression and the World Anti-Fascist War.
  • China, Russia conduct 10th joint strategic air patrol PRC MND 09 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese and Russian militaries conducted their 10th joint strategic air patrol in the relevant airspace over the East China Sea and the western part of the Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, according to a written statement of China's Ministry of National Defense. The joint patrol was carried out as part of the two militaries' annual cooperation plan, said the statement.

  • Operation Gateway in Malaysia strengthens regional security Australia DOD 09 Dec 2025 -- Over one of the world's busiest maritime corridors, Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) P-8A Poseidon crews collaborated with their Malaysian counterparts to enhance maritime domain awareness through Operation Gateway.
  • Funding boost for Australian-made Ghost Bat Australia DOD 09 Dec 2025 -- The Albanese Government will invest approximately $1.4 billion to advance collaborative air capabilities, transitioning the MQ-28A Ghost Bat into a fully operational war fighting asset for the Australian Defence Force.

  • ICG Ship Sarthak Reaches Suwaikh Port in Kuwait India PIB 09 Dec 2025 -- Indian Coast Guard (ICG) Ship Sarthak, an indigenously designed and built Offshore Patrol Vessel (OPV), arrived at Suwaikh Port in Kuwait on December 09, 2025, as part of its Overseas Deployment (OSD) to the Gulf region
  • Inaugural meeting of India-Brunei Joint Working Group on Defence Cooperation held in New Delhi to further strengthen bilateral ties India PIB 09 Dec 2025 -- The inaugural meeting of India-Brunei Joint Working Group (JWG) on Defence Cooperation took place in New Delhi on December 09, 2025, marking a significant milestone in advancing the bilateral partnership between the two nations
  • CHIEF OF THE NAVAL STAFF VISIT TO BRAZIL India PIB 09 Dec 2025 -- Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi, Chief of the Naval Staff (CNS), Indian Navy, has proceeded on an official visit to Brazil from 09 to 12 Dec 2025

  • Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, Chief of Army Staff & Chief of Defence Forces called on President of the Republic of Indonesia, His Excellency Prabowo Subianto at Islamabad. ISPR Pakistan 09 Dec 2025

  • US copying of Iranian drone was an admission of Tehran's superiority: Cmdr. IRNA 09 Dec 2025 -- The Iranian Armed Forces spokesperson says Washington's replication of the Shahed-136 drone amounts to an acknowledgment of Tehran's superiority in unmanned aerial vehicle technology.
  • Replication of Shahed-136 by US shows Iran's drone superiority: Official Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- The spokesman for the Iranian Armed Forces says the replication of Shahed-136 loitering munition attack drone by the United States validates the growing global recognition of the Islamic Republic's superiority in the development of unmanned aerial vehicles.

  • HRH the Crown Prince Inaugurates New Facilities at King Salman Air Base SPA 09 Dec 2025 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, inaugurated new facilities at King Salman Air Base in the Central Sector today as part of strategic projects aimed at enhancing the combat readiness of the Royal Saudi Air Force (RSAF)

  • Boost for defence businesses and European cooperation as UK joins defence exports treaty UK MOD 09 Dec 2025 -- The UK has joined a defence exports treaty with European allies which will make it easier for UK defence businesses to export
  • Partnerships with Azerbaijan and Armenia boosted through Defence Minister Lord Coaker visit UK MOD 09 Dec 2025 -- Defence Minister Lord Vernon Coaker visits Armenia and Azerbaijan, boosting defence ties.

  • President: Strategic Partnership between Bulgaria and the US Opens up Prospects for Achieving Specific Results in the Areas of Investment, Energy, and Defence President of the Republic of Bulgaria 09 Dec 2025 -- Head of State receives Christopher Smith, Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for Europe and Eurasia at the US Department of State

  • Enhanced bilateral defence cooperation between Romania and Germany Romania - Ministry of National Defence 09 Dec 2025 -- On Tuesday, December 9th, State Secretary for Defence Policy, Planning and International Relations, Sorin-Dan Moldovan, had a bilateral meeting with the Parliamentary State Secretary to the Ministry of Defence of the Federal Republic of Germany, Dr. Nils Schmid, on the occasion of his visit to Romania, at the 57th Mihail Kogălniceanu Air Base, Constanța County.

  • Finnish Border Guard's powers related to preparedness to be strengthened Finnish Government 09 Dec 2025 -- The Government proposes improvements to the Finnish Border Guard's preparedness and its ability to respond to the requirements of the changed security and operating environment.

  • International conference in Lublin, Poland, discusses security challenges in the Baltic Sea region Latvia MFA 09 Dec 2025 -- On 8 December 2025, Raimonds Jansons, Latvian Ambassador to Poland, at the international scientific conference "Poland and Latvia in the 20th and 21st Centuries: Parallel Developments, Different Experiences.

  • Key Takeaways As White House, Congress Push Foreign Policy Plans RFE/RL 09 Dec 2025 -- Two key documents released in Washington in recent days present differing foreign policy paths, giving US allies and adversaries alike plenty to chew over as they consider major strategic issues.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Egypt Foreign Minister Abdelatty US Dept. of State 09 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud US Dept. of State 09 Dec 2025

  • Contracts For Dec. 9, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 9 December 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 09 Dec 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israeli attacks continue in Gaza in violation of ceasefire; two more Palestinians killed Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- Two more Palestinian civilians have been killed and several others wounded as Israeli forces continue deadly aggression across the besieged Gaza Strip in violation of a recent ceasefire deal.

  • Hamas rules out 2nd phase of Gaza ceasefire while Israeli violations continue Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- A senior Hamas official says continued Israeli "violations" of the Gaza ceasefire have left no ground to begin phase-two negotiations, warning that progress is impossible until mediators force Israel to fully comply with the first stage.

  • Israeli Army Claims Strikes on Hezbollah Military Targets in Southern Lebanon Sputnik 09 Dec 2025 -- The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said that it had struck Hezbollah military targets in southern Lebanon.
  • 'No evidence': UNIFIL chief refutes Israeli claims about Hezbollah rearming in south Lebanon Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- The commander of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) says the multinational peacekeeping mission has not found any evidence of Hezbollah rebuilding its military capabilities, contradicting Israeli claims used as the pretext to strike southern Lebanon.
  • Israeli airstrikes disrupt fragile ceasefire in southern Lebanon Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- Israel continues to violate the ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, carrying out new airstrikes on various areas in the southern part of the Arab country

  • Born vulnerable: the toll of maternal malnutrition and stress in Gaza UNICEF 09 Dec 2025 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF Communication Manager Tess Ingram - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva
  • Gaza's babies 'scarred by war before first breath' by malnutrition UN News 09 Dec 2025 -- Mothers who've been left starving in Gaza are now giving birth to underweight or premature babies who die in intensive care units or struggle to survive as they endure acute malnutrition, the UN Children's Fund (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.
  • UN: 'Shockingly high' number of Gaza children still acutely malnourished despite ceasefire Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund (UNICEF) has warned that thousands of children in Gaza are acutely malnourished, driven by Israel's ongoing restrictions on food and humanitarian supplies despite a ceasefire agreement that was supposed to allow aid to move freely.

  • UK to boost peacebuilding efforts for Israel and Palestine UK FCDO 09 Dec 2025 -- The UK will host a key peacebuilding conference next year to help establish an International Peace Fund for Israel and Palestine.
  • Aboul Gheit condemns Israeli forces' raid on UNRWA HQ in East Jerusalem League of Arab States 09 Dec 2025 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, strongly condemned the Israeli forces' raid on the headquarters of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA) in Jerusalem
  • No: 248, 9 December 2025, Regarding the Raid on the UNRWA Compound in East Jerusalem by Israeli Security Forces Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025
  • Israel's new settlement expansion plan triggers global condemnation Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- Israel's decision to allocate $810 million over five years for the establishment of 17 new settlements and the expansion of existing ones in the occupied West Bank has triggered a wave of global condemnation, with Palestinian leaders, Arab states, the United Nations, the European Union, and several Western governments denouncing the move as a severe blow to peace prospects and a violation of international law.

  • OIC Condemns Israeli Security Forces' Storming of UNRWA Headquarters in Occupied Jerusalem Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 09 Dec 2025 -- The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) condemned in the strongest terms the Israeli occupation forces' storming of UNRWA headquarters in the Sheikh Jarah District in the occupied City of Jerusalem and lowering the United Nations flag on one of its properties

  • Tony Blair 'dropped' from Gaza 'Board of Peace' shortlist; Hamas welcomes move as 'step in right direction' Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- Former UK prime minister Tony Blair has reportedly been removed from consideration for US President Donald Trump-proposed so-called Gaza "Board of Peace," a development Hamas has welcomed as a "step in the right direction."

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Central Emergency Response Fund, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine UNOCHA 09 Dec 2025

  • Secretary-General's message on the International Day of Commemoration and Dignity of the Victims of the Crime of Genocide and the Prevention of this Crime UN Secretary-General 09 Dec 2025

  • UN seeks $1 billion for 'first-responder fund' in emergencies UN News 09 Dec 2025 -- Countries rallied at UN Headquarters on Tuesday to shore up a key humanitarian fund that provides timely assistance for millions worldwide when conflict and climate-related disasters strike.
  • UN calls for stepped-up commitments to global emergency fund as needs outstrip resources UNOCHA 09 Dec 2025 -- As humanitarian crises around the world outpace the funding available to address them, senior United Nations officials today rallied the international community to urgently mobilize more support for the UN Central Emergency Response Fund (CERF), at an annual pledging event marking the Fund's 20th anniversary.
  • UN Relief Chief: If CERF falters, people who need emergency aid will suffer UNOCHA 09 Dec 2025 -- Remarks at the High-Level Pledging Event for the Central Emergency Response Fund by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator

  • 2 major Chinese naval formations detected near PH EEZ Philippine News Agency 09 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine military has monitored two major Chinese naval formations near the eastern seaboard of Luzon and outside the country's exclusive economic zone (EEZ), an official said Tuesday.
  • 20 Chinese coast guard, naval ships spotted in WPS early December Philippine News Agency 09 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) has monitored 20 Chinese coast guard and naval ships in four critical areas of the West Philippine Sea (WPS) during the first week of December, most of them in Bajo de Masinloc and Ayungin Shoal.
  • Chinese envoy to UN refutes erroneous remarks by certain countries, reiterates China's position on South China Sea Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- A Chinese envoy to the UN said at a United Nations session on Monday local time that the General Assembly is not the proper forum for addressing the South China Sea issue, but said it must firmly counter "erroneous" remarks by certain countries.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Yemen UN Secretary-General 09 Dec 2025

  • 3 civilians injured as Israeli forces carry out ground incursion into southern Syria Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- At least three people have been wounded when Israeli military forces opened fire on them as they carried out a new ground incursion into Syria's southwestern province of Quneitra amid daily violations of Syrian sovereignty.
  • HRH the Crown Prince Speaks with Syrian President SPA 09 Dec 2025 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, today spoke by telephone with President Ahmed al-Sharaa of the Syrian Arab Republic

  • Armed clashes between Thailand and Cambodia continue, arousing regional security concerns Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- Armed clashes between Cambodia and Thailand continued on Tuesday with both sides issuing early-morning updates on exchange of fire and casualties, drawing widespread concern.
  • China will continue to play a constructive role in easing tensions in its own way, says FM spokesperson on Thailand-Cambodia flare-up Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- As a friend and close neighbor to both Cambodia and Thailand, China sincerely hopes that the two sides will exercise restraint, meet each other halfway, and prevent the situation from escalating further, said Chinese FM Spokesperson Guo Jiakun on Tuesday when asked to comment on the renewed escalation of tensions between Thailand and Cambodia, as well as reports that Thai fighter jets struck Cambodian artillery positions on Monday.

  • Director General's Statement on Publication of the Kenova Report mi5 09 Dec 2025 -- Director General Sir Ken McCallum made the following statement in response to the publication of the final Kenova report.

  • Myanmar: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union on the upcoming elections EC/CEU 09 Dec 2025

  • On Fighting on the Cambodia-Thailand Border US Dept. of State 09 Dec 2025 -- "The United States is concerned by continued fighting and casualties in multiple locations along the Cambodia-Thailand border."
  • Statement on Cambodia-Thailand border Australia DFAT 09 Dec 2025
  • US Urges End to Cambodia-Thailand Fighting, Calls for Peace Accords Implementation - Rubio Sputnik 09 Dec 2025 -- The United States is concerned by ongoing clashes and casualties along the Cambodia-Thailand border and urges an immediate halt to hostilities and a return to the de-escalation measures established in the October 26 Kuala Lumpur Peace Accords, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in a statement on Tuesday.
  • Thailand Strikes Cambodian City of Samraong Using F-16 Jets - Reports Sputnik 09 Dec 2025 -- Thailand carried out an airstrike on Tuesday targeting the center of the Cambodian city of Samraong in Oddar Meanchey province using F-16 fighter jets, Cambodian newspaper Khmer Times reported.

  • International Contact Group for the Great Lakes (ICG) on the escalation in South Kivu Germany Federal Foreign Office 09 Dec 2025 -- Belgium, Denmark, the European Union, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and the United States of America, as members of the International Contact Group for the Great Lakes (ICG), chaired by Germany, issue the following statement on the alarming escalation in South Kivu:

  • Ali Muhammad Ali Abd-Al-Rahman ('Ali Kushayb') Sentenced to 20 Years of Imprisonment ICC 09 Dec 2025 -- Today, 9 December 2025, Trial Chamber I of the International Criminal Court ("ICC" or "the Court") sentenced Ali Muhammad Ali Abd-Al-Rahman ('Ali Kushayb') to 20 years of imprisonment following the Trial Judgment in which the Chamber found him guilty of 27 counts of crimes against humanity and war crimes committed in Darfur, Sudan, between August 2003 and April 2004.

  • 'All we want for Sudan is peace' say children fleeing violence UN News 09 Dec 2025 -- Nahed was visiting Sudan's capital Khartoum with her family to celebrate Eid, a major Islamic holiday, when the war broke out between the rival armies vying for control of her homeland.
  • Sanctioning a Transnational Network Fueling the Conflict in Sudan US Dept. of State 09 Dec 2025 -- On November 19, President Trump detailed the horrific atrocities occurring in Sudan. The United States will use its power and influence to halt them.
  • Treasury Sanctions Transnational Network Recruiting Colombians to Fight in Sudan's Civil War US Treasury 09 Dec 2025 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on four individuals and four entities for their roles in fueling the civil war in Sudan, a conflict that has provoked the world's worst ongoing humanitarian crisis. This transnational network—primarily composed of Colombian nationals and companies—recruits former Colombian military personnel and trains soldiers, including children, to fight for the Sudanese paramilitary group known as the Rapid Support Forces (RSF).
  • Sudan must address ethnic violence and prevent further escalation, UN committee warns OHCHR 09 Dec 2025 -- UN anti-racism experts today underscored their grave concerns over the increase in the use of dehumanizing language, hate speech as well as ethnically motivated human rights violations and abuses in Sudan, particularly those targeting members belonging to the Fur, Masalit, and Zaghawa ethnic communities committed by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and allied forces in El Fasher, North Darfur.
  • UNICEF Executive Director warns of deepening protection crisis in Sudan as violence and displacement intensify UNICEF 09 Dec 2025 -- With Sudan facing one of the world's most severe humanitarian crises, including escalating conflict, rising hunger, and the highest level of child displacement in the world, UNICEF Executive Director Catherine Russell called for urgent action to safeguard children and essential services on her visit to the country.

  • Statement by Andrii Sybiha regarding the AP investigation about Russian atrocities in Mali Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 09 Dec 2025

News Reports

  • Trump's son-in-law part of Israeli-tied firm's bid for CNN's parent company Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, has emerged as one of the outside investors backing Paramount Skydance's hostile bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery, a deal that could hand partial ownership of CNN to the Trump family.

  • Israel conducting surveillance on US forces, allies at southern command center: Report Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- A recent report has revealed that Israeli operatives are conducting widespread surveillance of American troops and allies stationed at a new US command center in the southern part of the Israeli-occupied territories, with the US military and allied forces expressing concern over the activities.

  • United States Department of Justice Transfers 14 Mexican Nationals with Drug or Firearm Convictions to Mexico Under International Prisoner Transfer Treaty US Dept. of Justice 09 Dec 2025 -- The United States Justice Department's Office of International Affairs, with support from the Department's Federal Bureau of Prisons (BOP), transferred 14 Mexican nationals on Friday to Mexican authorities pursuant to the International Prisoner Transfer Treaty between the United States and Mexico.

  • Justice Department Announces Actions to Combat Two Russian State-Sponsored Cyber Criminal Hacking Groups US Dept. of Justice 09 Dec 2025 -- The Justice Department announced two indictments in the Central District of California charging Ukrainian national Victoria Eduardovna Dubranova, 33, also known as Vika, Tory, and SovaSonya, for her role in conducting cyberattacks and computer intrusions against critical infrastructure and other victims around the world, in support of Russia's geopolitical interests.
  • NSA, FBI, and Others Call Out Pro-Russia Hacktivist Groups Targeting Critical Infrastructure NSA / Central Security Service 09 Dec 2025 -- The National Security Agency (NSA) is joining the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) and over 20 others to release the Cybersecurity Advisory (CSA), "Pro-Russia Hacktivists Conduct Opportunistic Attacks Against US and Global Critical Infrastructure," and provide recommended mitigations to reduce the likelihood and impact of related incidents.
  • CISA, FBI, and U.S. and Global Partners Urge Immediate Action to Defend Critical Infrastructure from Pro-Russia Hacktivist Threats CISA 09 Dec 2025 -- Joint Cybersecurity Advisory Warns of Organized Threat Actors Seeking Targets of Opportunity Across All Critical Infrastructure Sectors

  • ESTABLISHMENT OF CYBER CRIME UNITS India PIB 09 Dec 2025 -- 'Police' and 'Public Order' are State subjects as per the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India.

  • COUNTER-TERRORISM MEASURES India PIB 09 Dec 2025 -- During the last two years, the Government has undertaken various major counter-terrorism measures, focusing on intelligence enhancement, inter-agency coordination, addressing emerging threats through online radicalization & encrypted digital platforms and strengthening of border security, which have significantly strengthened the capacity of Central and State law enforcement agencies for mitigation of terror risks

  • UK clamps down on China-based companies for reckless and irresponsible activity in cyberspace UK FCDO 09 Dec 2025 -- Two tech companies based in China have been sanctioned for reckless and indiscriminate cyberattacks

  • Deputy Foreign Minister Dmitry Lyubinsky's address at the ceremonial event at the UN Information Centre on the Occasion of Human Rights Day, Moscow, December 9, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025
  • Press release on Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's consultations on international and regional issues in Manama Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade of Hungary Peter Szijjarto Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during talks with Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade of Hungary Peter Szijjarto, Moscow, December 9, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025
  • Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov's interview with the magazine International Affairs, Moscow, December 8, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on December 9, 2025 PRC MofA 09 Dec 2025
  • Joint Press Release of the Third Meeting of the China-Iran-Saudi Arabia Trilateral Joint Committee PRC MofA 09 Dec 2025 -- The third meeting of the China-Iran-Saudi Arabia Trilateral Joint Committee was held in Tehran on December 9, 2025, to follow up on the implementation of the Beijing Agreement.
  • Hong Kong: UN rights chief voices concern over 'draconian' laws, in wake of deadly apartment fire UN News 09 Dec 2025 -- UN human rights chief Volker Türk has voiced concern over reports that "draconian security laws" in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of China are being used to clampdown on people calling for a probe into the deadly fire at an apartment complex last month.
  • Former executive of Chinese financial firm Bai Tianhui lawfully executed for accepting bribes worth over 1.108 billion yuan Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- A former executive of a Chinese financial firm Bai Tianhui was lawfully executed by the Tianjin No.2 Intermediate People's Court on Tuesday morning, following approval by the Supreme People's Court, according to a statement released on the official WeChat account of the Tianjin High People's Court.
  • HK election hailed as a milestone for democracy People's Daily 09 Dec 2025 -- The central authorities and various sectors of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region hailed on Monday the formation of the new-term Legislative Council as a further demonstration of the SAR's high-quality democracy and a guarantee of the smooth implementation of the "one country, two systems" policy.
  • China launches new internet satellite group Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- China sent a new satellite group into space on Tuesday from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in north China's Shanxi Province.

  • Premier faces lawsuit, potential censure motion over revenue allocation law CNA 09 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's opposition parties on Tuesday ramped up pressure on Premier Cho Jung-tai (卓榮泰) over his remark suggesting the Executive Yuan might ignore legislation on central-local government revenue allocation.
  • Wang Yi: The Status of Taiwan Has Been "Sealed with Seven Locks" PRC MofA 09 Dec 2025 -- On December 8, 2025, Wang Yi, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister, set forth the historical facts and legal bases of the Taiwan question during talks with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul in Beijing.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 09 Dec 2025
  • All countries have responsibility, obligation to join hands in resisting attempt to revive militaristic fascism: Chinese FM on regional countries opposing Takaichi's Taiwan remarks Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- Since modern times, Japanese militarism, under the pretext of so-called "survival-threatening situation", launched aggressive wars against China and other Asian neighbors, committing all manner of atrocities wherever it went, leaving behind horrifying blood debts, the Chinese FM spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday in response to a question about neighboring countries such as Russia have recently voiced opposition to the erroneous Taiwan-related remarks made by Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi
  • Taiwan's status 'locked in by seven layers of confirmation'; pursuit or support of independence violates constitution and international law: Chinese FM Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- Attempts to pursue "Taiwan independence" mean splitting China's territory, and supporting "Taiwan independence" amounts to interference in China's internal affairs, which violates both China's constitution and international law, said Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing on Monday, stressing that Taiwan's status has been "secured under seven layers of confirmation."
  • Taiwan's status 'locked in by seven layers of confirmation'; pursuit or support of independence violates constitution and international law: Chinese FM Global Times 09 Dec 2025 -- Attempts to pursue "Taiwan independence" mean splitting China's territory, and supporting "Taiwan independence" amounts to interference in China's internal affairs, which violates both China's constitution and international law, said Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing on Monday, stressing that Taiwan's status has been "secured under seven layers of confirmation."
  • Supporting "Taiwan independence" violates China's constitution, international law: Chinese FM People's Daily 09 Dec 2025 -- Attempts to pursue "Taiwan independence" mean splitting China's territory, and supporting "Taiwan independence" amounts to interference in China's internal affairs, which violates both China's constitution and international law, said Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing on Monday.

  • DPRK Delegation of Ministry of Disaster Management Leaves for Russia KCNA 09 Dec 2025 -- The delegation of the Ministry of Disaster Management headed by Vice-Minister Ri Ki Chol left here on December 8 by air to visit Russia.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Sends Condolences to Russian President and Sympathies to Russian Embassy KCNA 09 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, sent a message of condolence to Vladimir Vladimirovich Putin, president of the Russian Federation, on December 8 as regards the demise of Aleksandr Ivanovich Matsegora, Russian extraordinary ambassador to the DPRK, on December 6, 2025.

  • ROK-PNG Framework Agreement for Grant Aid Signed ROK MFA 09 Dec 2025
  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (December 9, 2025) ROK MFA 09 Dec 2025

  • China reiterates support for efforts to strengthen Iran-Saudi ties IRNA 09 Dec 2025 -- The People's Republic of China has expressed its readiness to continue supporting and encouraging efforts made by the Islamic Republic of Iran and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to enhance their relations across various fields.
  • Exclusive I IAEA safeguards inadequate for post-war conditions: AEOI spokesperson IRNA 09 Dec 2025 -- Behrouz Kamalvandi, spokesperson and deputy head of Iran's Atomic Energy Organization (AEOI), says the International Atomic Energy Agency's (IAEA) current safeguards agreement is inadequate in light of the circumstances following the 12‑day war imposed by Israel, and therefore requires legal revision.
  • Nuclear chief: Adhering to IAEA rules essential to prevent accusations of non-compliance IRNA 09 Dec 2025 -- Iran's nuclear chief says the country will continue advancing its civilian nuclear program while fully observing the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA)'s frameworks to avoid politically motivated accusations about its activities.

  • India - U.S. Foreign Office Consultations (December 09, 2025) India MEA 09 Dec 2025 -- The India - United States Foreign Office Consultations (FOC), co-chaired by Foreign Secretary Shri Vikram Misri and U.S. Under Secretary of State Ms. Allison Hooker were held in New Delhi earlier today.

  • Visit of H.E. Prabowo Subianto, President of the Republic of Indonesia, to Pakistan (8-9 December 2025) - Joint Statement Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Dec 2025 -- At the invitation of H.E. Mr. Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, H.E. Mr. Prabowo Subianto, President of the Republic of Indonesia, paid an official visit to Pakistan on 8-9 December 2025.

  • The Foreign Secretary's Locarno Centenary Speech UK FCDO 09 Dec 2025 -- To commemorate the centenary of the signing of the Locarno Treaties in London, the Foreign Secretary delivered a speech on the lessons from 100 years ago and the new threats we face today.

  • Belarus Accused Of Sabotaging Lithuanian Airspace As EU Moves Toward New Sanctions RFE/RL 09 Dec 2025 -- The European Union is finally moving closer to sanctioning Belarus for the release of weather balloons over Lithuania in recent months.

  • Opening remarks by High Representative/Vice President Kaja Kallas for the Extraordinary Meeting and Exchange of Views with the European Parliament's Committee on Foreign Affairs European External Action Service (EEAS) 09 Dec 2025
  • Opening remarks by High Representative/Vice President Kaja Kallas at the 26th EU-NGO Human Rights Forum European External Action Service (EEAS) 09 Dec 2025

  • The 2026 Federal Foreign Office budget - setting the course for strong foreign policy Germany Federal Foreign Office 09 Dec 2025 -- With a total budget of around 6 billion euro for 2026, the Federal Foreign Office is facing up to global challenges and prioritising foreign policy that remains resolute and capable even in difficult times.

  • Statement by Minister Valtonen at the UN Security Council meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Finland 09 Dec 2025 -- Statement by the OSCE Chairperson-in-Office, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Finland Elina Valtonen at the United Nations Security Council meeting in New York, 8 December 2025.

  • Budrys: EU enlargement - Europe's most important geopolitical instrument Lithuania MFA 09 Dec 2025 -- On 9 December, Minister of Foreign Affairs Kęstutis Budrys met with the European Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos, who is visiting Lithuania. During the meeting, the Minister and the European Commissioner discussed the European Union's enlargement policy, with particular attention given to the prospects of Ukraine's and Moldova's EU membership.
  • The President at a meeting with EU Commissioner: EU Enlargement - a Guarantee for Europe's Security President of the Republic of Lithuania 09 Dec 2025 -- President Gitanas Nausėda met with European Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos.

  • OSCE Chair calls for respect for shared principles and strengthening multilateralism in her address to the UN Security Council OSCE 09 Dec 2025 -- In her address to the United Nations Security Council, OSCE Chairperson-in-Office, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Finland Elina Valtonen urged respect for shared principles and the strengthening of multilateralism, calling for renewed commitment to the values enshrined in both the UN Charter and the Helsinki Final Act.

  • Honduras issues arrest warrant for ex-president recently pardoned by Trump Press TV 09 Dec 2025 -- Honduras's top prosecutor has issued an arrest warrant for former president Juan Orlando Hernández, who was freed from US federal prison last week after being pardoned by President Donald Trump.

  • China-Backed Bridge Project In Belgrade Draws Scrutiny Over Hidden Contracts RFE/RL 09 Dec 2025 -- Construction has begun to replace Serbia's iconic Sava Bridge, but the government's refusal to fully disclose its agreements with the Chinese companies involved is fueling opposition to the project and raising transparency questions over how the contracts were awarded.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Hitting Belarusian Balloons And Montenegro's EU Prospects RFE/RL 09 Dec 2025

  • Albares presents new Strategy for Asia-Pacific Spain MFA 09 Dec 2025 -- The Council of Ministers approves the document that commits to increasing Spain's presence in the region
